Respect must be paid to the dignity of age and position. The younger people, if they are wrong, must be corrected, not because we want to find fault with them, but because we are willing to make the best of them. There is a need for much gentleness and care in correcting those who deserve correction.

Honor widows who are truly widows, help them, and support them. It is the duty of children, if their parents are in need and they are able to help them, to do so to the fullest extent of their ability. Widowhood is a desolate state; but let widows trust in the Lord, and continue in prayer. All who live in pleasure are spiritually dead while they live, dead in transgressions and sins. Sadly, there are many people like this among nominal Christians, even until the end of life! If any men or women do not support their poor relatives, they effectively deny the faith. If they spend money on their desires and pleasures that should be used to support their families, they have denied the faith, and are worse than unbelievers. If those who profess the gospel give way to any corrupt principle or behavior, they are worse than those who do not profess to believe in the doctrines of grace.

Everyone brought into an office in the church should be free from just criticism; and many are proper objects of charity, yet ought not to be employed in public services. Those who would find mercy when they are in distress must show mercy when they are prosperous; and those who demonstrate readiness for every good work are most likely to be faithful with whatever is entrusted to them. Those who are idle very seldom are only idle; they make trouble among neighbors, and sow discord among brothers. All believers are required to help those belonging to their families who are without resources, so that the church may not be prevented from helping those who are entirely destitute and friendless.

Care must be taken that ministers are supported. And those who work diligently in this task are worthy of double honor and respect. It is their just due, as much as the reward of a laborer. The apostle instructs Timothy solemnly to guard against partiality. We have great need to watch at all times so we do not share in other people's sins. Keep yourself pure, not only from doing such things yourself, but from approving of them or in any way helping with them in others. The apostle also instructs Timothy to take care of his health. As we are not to make our bodies masters, so neither slaves; but to use them so that they may be most helpful to us in the service of God. There are secret sins, and there are open sins: some people’s sins are evident beforehand, and go before unto judgment; some follow after. God will bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and make known the intentions of all hearts. Looking forward to the day of judgment, let us all attend to our proper duties, whether in higher or lower positions, striving that the name and teachings of God may never be blasphemed because of us.
